title: Neumann boundary equalities make the heat boundary term vanish

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the left ghost value equals the first interior value and the right ghost value equals the last interior value, then the heat boundary term is zero.
assumptions: u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n is a natural-number range length
informal.expected_conclusion: The heat boundary term vanishes under zero-gradient boundary equalities.

declarations.name: heatBoundaryTerm
declarations.statement: def heatBoundaryTerm (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := u 0 - u 1 + (u (n + 1) - u n)

declarations.name: heatBoundaryTerm_eq_zero_of_neumann
declarations.statement: theorem heatBoundaryTerm_eq_zero_of_neumann (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hLeft : u 0 = u 1) (hRight : u (n + 1) = u n) : heatBoundaryTerm u n = 0

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation total-balance theorem into Lean 4: If the left ghost value equals the first interior value and the right ghost value equals the last interior value, then the heat boundary term is zero.
tasks.target: theorem heatBoundaryTerm_eq_zero_of_neumann (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hLeft : u 0 = u 1) (hRight : u (n + 1) = u n) : heatBoundaryTerm u n = 0

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding heatBoundaryTerm, rewriting the boundary equalities, and using ring.
